title: RIO Cross-Substrate Handoff
description: Defines the boundary protocol when execution crosses from one substrate to another. Receipt validation does not grant execution permission.
name: Cross-Substrate Handoff
rule: Receipt validation does not grant execution permission

validate_upstream_receipt.description: Validate the receipt from the upstream substrate. Four checks: signature, timing, identity, measurement.

validate_compatibility.description: Verify that the upstream receipt's action, target, and parameters are compatible with the downstream substrate's capabilities and policy.
validate_compatibility.on_failure: BLOCK. Incompatible substrates cannot hand off.

issue_new_dtt.description: Issue a NEW Delegation Trust Token (DTT) for the downstream substrate. This is a fresh authorization — not derived from the upstream receipt.

properties.scope: Local to the downstream substrate only.
properties.ttl: Bounded. Same TTL rules as any execution token.
properties.source: Issued by the gate, not by the upstream substrate.

execute.description: Execute the action in the downstream substrate under the newly issued DTT. Standard kernel execution rules apply.
execute.requires: Valid DTT from the previous step.

generate_receipt.description: Generate a receipt for the downstream execution. This receipt can be validated by the next boundary in the chain.

no_implicit_authority_flow.description: Authority does not flow implicitly from one substrate to another. A valid upstream receipt is necessary but not sufficient for downstream execution.

receipt_not_permission.description: A receipt is proof of what happened. It is not permission for what happens next. Receipts never grant authority.

new_authorization_required.description: Every cross-substrate handoff requires a new, locally issued authorization (DTT). No exceptions.

upstream_receipt_signature.description: The upstream receipt must have a valid signature from the issuing authority.

upstream_receipt_timing.description: The upstream receipt must be within the expected time window. Not expired. Not future-dated.

upstream_receipt_identity.description: The identity that produced the upstream receipt must match the expected actor.

upstream_receipt_measurement.description: The receipt hash must cover the actual execution output. Recomputation must match.

new_dtt_independence.description: The new DTT is issued by the gate. It is not derived from, signed by, or dependent on the upstream receipt beyond the validation check.

failure_conditions.upstream_receipt_invalid: Any of the 4 receipt checks fail → BLOCK. No downstream action.
failure_conditions.compatibility_check_fails: Upstream action incompatible with downstream substrate → BLOCK.
failure_conditions.no_new_dtt_issued: Execution attempted without a fresh DTT → BLOCK.
failure_conditions.upstream_receipt_used_as_authorization: Receipt treated as permission instead of evidence → VIOLATION. This is the core anti-pattern this spec prevents.
